chapter.label: Tale 119
chapter.title: The demon disguised as a monk WHICH

paragraphs.text: CAME AT THE CALL OF AN ELDER AT RAITHOU [The fathers of the same place told us concerning Abba Gregory of H Byzantium and his disciple, Abba Gregory from Paras, that they When we met Abba Eusebios, priest of the Javra at Raithou, he told stayed on an island in the Red Sea. Now there was no water on that that a demon once arrived at an elder’s cell disguised as a monk. He H island: they used to bring the water to supply their needs from the knocked at the door: the elder opened up to him and said: ‘Pray’. i mainland. They had a boat in which they would set out and bring The demon said: ‘Now and for ever and unto the ages of ages, | back water. Onc day they put the boat in the water and tied it to a amen’. Three times the elder said: ‘Pray’, and each time the demon ! stone. That night a great wave came and broke the rope, sending said: ‘Now and for ever and unto the ages of ages, amen". The cider H the boat to the bottom. Now the fathers were left with no way of then said: "You arc welcome il when you pray you say: “Glory be i providing themselves with water. Eight months Jater, some monks to the Father and to the Son and to the Holy Ghost, as it was in i came from Raithou and found iliem both dead. They also found a the beginning, is now and ever shall be, sow and for ever and unto ! tortoise-shell with this written on it: ‘Abba Gregory of Pharon died the ages of ages, amen”, and when the elder said this, the demon | after going twenty-eight days without drinking water, bul I have disappeared as though it were pursued by fire’. gone thirty-seven days without a drink". We found both the corpses intact: we took them back and buried them at Raithou.®]
